title: demo(ardop): raise ARQ transfer timeout so the message payload completes under non-ideal channels
description: The live ARDOP connected-mode demo (demo/ardop) fails to fully deliver the default ~3.3KB SNDM message (SITREP text + tower JPEG) in any condition other than Ideal. Root cause: server.py builds SessionParams without overriding data_timeout, so the transfer window is the dataclass default of 90s (testbench.py:269) and ardopcf is told ARQTIMEOUT 90 (testbench.py:345). At the demo's effective HF throughput (~440 bps on Ideal per the 2895B/53s data point, less once the link rate-adapts down under Good/Moderate/Poor) a 3.3KB transfer needs minutes, so 90s only ever fits the pristine Ideal channel -> Good/Moderate/Poor end the loop with got<total -> 'partial'. Fix: raise data_timeout to 300s and ARQTIMEOUT to 240s (ardopcf's max; bounds verified in HostInterface.c: 30-240). Post-connect window only, so the SNR-15 no-connect path (60s CONNECT timeout) is unaffected. Operator-reported during PR #51 laptop verification.

title: Frame format: drop per-frame SRC/DST callsigns; conn_id addressing; ID at start/end/<=10min only
description: Operator correction (2026-06-14): putting SRC+DST callsigns (20 bytes) in EVERY frame is wasteful (~half the 42-byte header; larger than the payload on a deep-floor over) and is NOT Part-97 convention. Part 97 wants ID at the end of a communication and at least every 10 min (and by convention at the start), never per frame. CHANGE: (1) remove SRC/DST from the per-frame wire header; (2) conn.rs handle_frame addresses by conn_id (the negotiated session key), not callsign match; (3) callsigns ride only on ID-bearing frames: CONN/CONN_ACK (start), a periodic ID frame (<=10 min timer), DISC (end). Shrinks header ~42->~22 bytes. BREAKING wire change (pre-release). This is the FIRST link task next session, ahead of the downshift loop. See memory sonde-part97-id-at-start-end-not-per-frame.

title: LINK: Part-97 station ID (SRC+DST callsigns) transmitted in EVERY frame — wastes ~20 B/frame, not a Part 97 convention
description: LINK-LAYER ONLY (sonde-link, owned by the dedicated link agent — NOT a PHY issue; sonde-phy/phy-runtime/tx have no callsign logic). The LinkFrame wire format (crates/sonde-link/src/frame.rs:11-12) embeds SRC callsign (10 B) + DST callsign (10 B) in EVERY on-air frame = 20 B of station ID per frame, ~44% of the 45-B LINK_OVERHEAD. Part 97 (47 CFR 97.119) requires station ID only at the END of a communication and at least every 10 minutes during — NOT every frame. Per-frame callsigns waste airtime with no regulatory benefit. Convention: ID at the beginning and/or end of a transmission (over/session), not per-frame or per-subset. The frame already carries CONN_ID (2 B session id, frame.rs:13) which can address intermediate frames without repeating callsigns. Fix direction (link agent + operator to design): carry full SRC/DST callsigns only on opening (CONN/CONN_ACK) + closing (DISC) frames plus a >=10-min periodic ID; intermediate DATA/ACK frames identify by CONN_ID. Recovers ~20 B/frame. Found by bison-slate-gorge while investigating sonde-vb9 (PHY); flagged for the link agent per operator instruction not to touch the link layer from the PHY session.

title: PHY seams for link integration: PhyTransport::tx_in_flight() + DecodeScan (detected-but-failed)
description: The parallel link-layer agent (sonde-8xw) is BLOCKED on two honest PHY signals it needs for real-radio integration. (1) TX-in-flight: add PhyTransport::tx_in_flight()->usize (default 0); SondePhy increments on send_frame enqueue, decrements when Radio::transmit returns (PTT released). Lets the link freeze its turn-recovery clock on real keying instead of estimating from ModeProfile.over_airtime(). (2) Detected-but-failed-decode: change Waveform::decode_scan to return DecodeScan{NoSignal,Detected,Frame} so do_rx can bump frames_total on a detected-but-undecodable frame (today decode_scan returns None for both silence AND detected-but-FEC-failed, so ChannelQualityReport::frame_error_rate() structurally can't see RX failures -> no real link adaptation). title: PHY: rate-1/4 coded path loses ~6dB to per-symbol pilot channel estimation (mislabeled as FEC coding-gain bug)
description: Measured (sonde-xhw.4, aligned receive_multi over AWGN, my-Eb/N0 scale): coded rate-1/4 LDPC frame-error-rate threshold is ~13-14 dB, where uncoded (IdentityFec) BER is ALREADY ~1e-4. A good rate-1/4 LDPC at n=2048 should decode ~7 dB BELOW uncoded, not at parity. So the code provides essentially no net coding gain. Ruled OUT: the sonde-gtg n0 channel-estimate-error floor (disabling it left coded FER unchanged). Candidate causes: the IRA/dual-diagonal H construction (changed mid-flight in sonde-64w.1 due to rank-deficiency — may be a weak code), SPA decoder iteration count (MAX_ITERS), or LLR scaling/saturation into decode_soft. This blocks a credible Step 3 (xhw.4) coded-vs-uncoded coding-gain gate. Earlier 'decodes over fading' results were at high enough Eb/N0 that even a weak code works, so this was latent until the AWGN coding-gain measurement. Investigate: coded-vs-uncoded BER waterfall over AWGN; expect ~5-7 dB gain at 1e-4.
notes: CONVERGED FIX (Codex round 2): root cause is OFDM RX per-symbol pilot channel estimation, NOT FEC/n0/clamp/mirror. FIX: time-smooth pilot estimates across symbols (block-level demod) - centered 9-symbol triangular [1 2 3 4 5 4 3 2 1]/25 (~0.48s, -2dB@1Hz, safe for <=1Hz Doppler); EMA alpha~0.3-0.4 fallback. ~+4dB. Mirror MRC REJECTED (real-cast => freq[N-k]=conj(freq[k]) incl noise, not independent). oracle-vs-codec ~2.3dB = irreducible overhead (pilots1.31+CP0.97). Expected coded flat-AWGN threshold 9dB->~5.5dB. Step-3 gate target ~5.5-6dB NOT 3dB. Fix touches the demod the Watterson fading gate rides - regression-test fading; smoother must not smear Doppler. Full writeup: dev/findings-vb9-ldpc-coding-gain.md. Investigation artifacts (4 #[ignore] diagnostics) committed on sonde-vb9/ldpc-coding-gain. FIX NOT YET IMPLEMENTED.
close_reason: Fixed via PR #42 (merged). Root cause was PHY per-symbol pilot channel estimation, not FEC. Innovation-gated LMMSE pilot time-blend: coded flat-AWGN threshold ~12dB->~6dB, fading gate 16/16 (no regression), CI regression guard added. Full analysis dev/findings-vb9-ldpc-coding-gain.md.

title: Deep-robustness FT8-class floor — promote floor-nfsk to the MainAuto bottom rung
description: Extend the auto-adaptation ladder (ModeHint::MainAuto) BELOW floor-wblo with a genuine weak-signal mode: narrow (tens of Hz), slow (long symbols), strong FEC, very low rate — designed to approach FT8-class sensitivity, so the link degrades to a low-rate TRICKLE instead of failing. Promote/rebuild floor-nfsk from today's manual 'crowded-band' sidecar (ModeHint::FloorCrowdedBand -> NarrowFskFloor: uncoded noncoherent 8-FSK, 50 Hz spacing, 0.16 s symbols, NO FEC) into the bottom rung of MainAuto. Work: (1) add strong low-rate FEC (LDPC) to the FSK mode; (2) narrow the tone spacing / lengthen coherent integration toward FT8-class; (3) wire it into ModeHint::MainAuto resolution in crates/sonde-phy/src/modes.rs (MainAuto currently spans only the OFDM ladder + falls back to floor-wblo at low SNR -> add the deep floor as the lowest rung with SNR thresholds + hysteresis); (4) extend the ladder spec (overview Sec 5.A) accordingly. Physics-gated per the sonde-xhw reset: decode-rate-vs-SNR must be MEASURED in the standard 2.5 kHz reference, not loopback.
acceptance_criteria: A MEASURED decode-rate-vs-SNR curve, in the standard 2.5 kHz reference (SNR-in-2500-Hz, per P0 methodology), showing the deep-floor mode decodes within a STATED margin of FT8's ~-21 dB sensitivity. Loopback round-trip does NOT count.

title: Step 2: real synchronization in the loop — Schmidl-Cox preamble, CFO, Gardner, clock tracking
description: Ship a repeated-pair (Schmidl-Cox) preamble so the CFO estimator can run; wire CFO derotation; wire Gardner fractional-timing; add sample-clock tracking/resampling; complex matched filter for acquisition (reuse the parked sonde-64w.3 I/Q matched filter). Remove the hand-aligned bypass from the fading tests.
design: Codex-converged (towhee-slate-opossum review, 2026-06-14): (1) Preamble = Schmidl-Cox repeated pair [h|h], H=160 (320 total), ZC root coprime with 160 -> root 29 (25 has gcd 5). CFO unambiguous +-150 Hz. (2) Acquisition = full [h|h] I/Q-magnitude MF (single-half gives twin peaks -> half-preamble false lock); recalibrate threshold. (3) CFO: derotate analytic, take Re{}, feed existing real demod; pad Hilbert vs edge transients. (4) Timing = pilot-phase-SLOPE feedforward (differential, so channel group delay != timing), NOT Gardner (single-carrier tool, wrong for OFDM; keep symbol_timing.rs as FSK substrate). (5) NO resampler needed at gate scale (100ppm x 25.6k samples = 2.6 samp drift << 512 CP); window-tracking + phase-ramp equalizer suffice; resampler only for >27s frames -> defer to follow-up bd. (6) Gate Eb/N0: Good 14dB, Moderate 18dB (require Moderate); 30dB diagnostic only. SNR_2500 ~ Eb/N0-8.6dB. (7) clock injection in harness = band-limited windowed-sinc resampler on analytic, AWGN after. Eb/N0 knob: snr_db_arg = ebn0_db + 10log10(n_info_bits/buffer_len) [discrete-time conv, matches Step0]. Keep residual CFO <1-2Hz.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end decode over Watterson with injected carrier offset (+/-~100 Hz), sample-clock error (+/-ppm), and fractional timing, THROUGH the production sync path. Fading tests pass with sync in the loop or they do not count.

title: Symmetric SNR/route()-based link adaptation (supersede upshift probe)
description: Operator design correction (2026-06-15) superseding the upshift path landed in sonde-ruu (#45). The receiver must be AUTHORITATIVE for the rung in BOTH directions, not just downshift. The merged loop used a binary clean/missed quality window, which can authoritatively pick a downshift target (a miss = too fast, severity = how far) but cannot determine upshift DISTANCE from binary info (a clean decode only proves the current rung works, not the SNR margin above it) — which forced an unrealistic sender-side probe with consecutive-permit hysteresis (multiple half-duplex round-trips per rung). Fix: base the receiver's recommendation on a smoothed channel MEASUREMENT (SNR+FER) via the existing mac::route()/recommended_rung (which already maps quality->rung in both directions), authoritative up and down; the only hysteresis is a small SNR dead-band at rung boundaries (threshold hysteresis), no round-trip counting, no probe. Keep the sender's self-downshift-on-missed-reply (total loss = no measurement) + P1 BASE-fallback. Needs per-over SNR wired from the PHY channel_quality() (Driver/Link seam) into the recommendation; channel-model gates feed synthetic SNR. Real teeth await sonde-99l (PHY per-mode quality). Codex-converge before building. RADIO-1.

PRE-TUNED => a lost downshift announcement = deafness until the link's recovery-to-floor kicks in => correct but blunt (glitch -> floor -> climb). title: Floor demod: estimate noise variance for channel-aware LLR (replace hardcoded n0=0.1)
description: The wideband floor's channel-aware LLR (ofdm_main/receiver.rs) uses a fixed n0=0.1, calibrated for the high-SNR equalizer-isolation regime. Measured in sonde-xhw.3: the floor decodes Watterson Good/Moderate only above ~Eb/N0 25 dB; below that the LLR magnitudes are mis-scaled and the LDPC fails. title: SondePhy RX: streaming/ring-buffer decode across RX windows (frame > one capture window)
description: Codex adversarial review of the link-layer MVP (sonde-lcw) found: SondePhy::do_rx captures RX_WINDOW_SAMPLES=12000 (~0.25s) per idle window and calls Waveform::decode_scan on just that window. A floor frame is longer than one window (preamble + multiple 2560-sample OFDM symbols; one coded block needs >= symbols_per_block*2560 samples). A faithful half-duplex Radio honoring max_samples cannot deliver a full frame in one receive(); the runtime has no cross-window reassembly. LoopbackRadio currently masks this by returning the whole pending buffer ignoring max_samples (radio.rs:61). Needs: streaming/ring-buffer decode across windows, or a receive-until-frame API. Out of scope for the link MVP (which uses a single-over-burst test medium and documents the limitation); tracked here for the PHY-runtime owner.

title: Preamble correlator mislocates under channel phase rotation (complex matched filter)
description: The real-valued Zadoff-Chu preamble correlator (crates/sonde-phy/src/sync/preamble.rs) MISLOCATES the frame start under a complex/phase-rotated Watterson channel. Discovered during sonde-64w.2 (equalizer fix): with the channel-aware-LLR equalizer fix in place, the coded floor decodes 40/40 (Good) and 37/40 (Moderate) at KNOWN alignment (sync bypassed), but only 27/40 (Good) / 29/40 (Moderate) via the production receive_multi_with_sync path. EVERY production failure has perfect_align_ok=true, sync_ok=false -> the gap is purely the correlator landing the frame outside the +/-128-sample within-CP guard. Root cause: a real-only normalized matched filter loses correlation when the channel rotates the preamble ~90deg (Re-projection of a rotated analytic ZC is near-orthogonal to the real template), so it locks onto a spurious/delayed peak. FIX DIRECTION (converge with Codex before building, per standing directive): COMPLEX matched filter — correlate the analytic received signal against the complex ZC template and peak on |corr| (invariant to channel phase); optionally earliest-peak-within-multipath-window selection to align to the direct path. Then upgrade the fading gate's E2E assertions from converged-seed-only to a seed-robust set, and tighten the sync control to assert offset accuracy (not just detection.is_some).
notes:
Evidence captured in sonde-64w.2 PR. The equalizer fix (channel-aware LLR) is necessary but not sufficient for production fading robustness; this correlator fix is the second half. Prior 'late-sync->ISI' theory was wrong (timing was a symptom of correlator mislocation under rotation, not CP-overflow ISI).

title: Floor receiver lacks channel equalization — Watterson fading breaks decode
description: The wideband low-density floor decodes cleanly on AWGN but FAILS through ANY Watterson fading (even zero-Doppler/zero-delay), because it has no per-subcarrier channel estimation/equalizer — a static complex rotation defeats the preamble-sync phase reference. Surfaced wiring the FEC differential gate (sonde-64w.1), which is therefore AWGN-only. Needed before any fading-condition channel-sim characterization or on-air robustness. Likely pairs with pilot-aided equalization in the OFDM main family.
notes: ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED (bayou-crag-granite session, Codex-converged 2026-06-14): the prior 'late-sync->ISI' hypothesis is REFUTED by instrumentation (tests/zz_fading_instrument.rs). Evidence: Good detection.start_sample=0 (PERFECT, not 24 late); perfect-alignment decode (correlator bypassed) FAILS for Good+Moderate; decode-basin sweep [-320,+160] EMPTY. So timing (A1/A2/larger-G) cannot fix it. REAL cause: deep frequency-selective NULL from the 2-tap Watterson channel (pilot |Y| profile shows 16.5x spread Good / 27x Moderate, notch at ~4-6% of peak). The pilot-aided equalizer does ZERO-FORCING then feeds fixed scalar n0=0.1 to compute_llr -> at the null, ZF amplifies wrong-phase interp -> HIGH-confidence WRONG-sign LLRs (poison to SPA). ZF discards per-SC reliability the LDPC needs. Real rate-1/4 LDPC ALSO currently fails (CRC mismatch). FIX (converged): (1) channel-aware soft LLR (BPSK 4*Re(conj(H)*Y)/N0; general max-log -|Y-H*c|^2/N0) instead of ZF+fixed-n0; (2) fix equalizer edge-extrapolation (bins past last pilot default to 1+0j -- Wide bins 112,113 unestimated); (3) LLR clipping; (4) fading gate must use REAL FloorRate14Codec, not IdentityFec (uncoded-through-null = physics, not a bug). Seam: sonde-phy-runtime FloorWaveform::new still uses IdentityFec floor.
close_reason: Channel-aware soft LLR + equalizer edge-extrapolation fix landed (PR #24, merge f1f734e). Floor decodes through Watterson Good/Moderate fading with the real rate-1/4 LDPC. Root cause was frequency-selective null defeating ZF+fixed-n0 LLR (NOT the prior timing theory). Second defect (preamble correlator phase-robustness) split to sonde-64w.3.

title: Sync: fractional resampler for long-frame sample-clock error (>~27 s or high SRO)
description: sonde-xhw.3 established that at the floor's frame length (~1.5 s) and +-100 ppm, accumulated clock drift (~7 samples) is far inside the 512-sample CP and is absorbed by the per-symbol pilot equalizer's phase ramp — so NO explicit resampler is needed for the current gate (Codex-converged Q5). It becomes mandatory when accumulated drift approaches a CP-budget fraction (~CP/4=128 samples => ~27 s at 100 ppm) or for larger SRO that causes visible subcarrier-spacing ICI. Add pilot-phase-slope-tracked feedforward window repositioning and/or a fractional resampler then. Pairs with the pilot-aided timing tracking deferred here.
